entries.summary: Committed .architect-team-notify.json recipient config + shipped example file; absent config = silent no-op
scenarios: Valid config is loaded
Absent config is a silent no-op
Malformed config does not break the run
Shipped example config is itself valid
acceptance_criteria: scripts/notify/notify.py loads .architect-team-notify.json from the project root and parses provider, from_address, optional from_name, provider settings, and recipients[] (each with email + events[])
When the config file is absent the notifier sends nothing and exits 0 with no stderr output
When the config is invalid JSON or missing a required field the notifier writes a stderr warning, sends nothing, and exits 0
.architect-team-notify.example.json exists at the repo root, parses as JSON, and contains every required field

entries.summary: Stdlib-only Gmail (SMTP) + SendGrid (HTTP API) providers selected by config; secrets from env vars only
scenarios: Gmail provider builds an authenticated SMTP message
SendGrid provider builds an authenticated API request
Missing provider secret is handled gracefully
Secret value is never disclosed
acceptance_criteria: A provider abstraction with GmailProvider and SendGridProvider is selected by the config provider field
GmailProvider transmits via smtp.gmail.com over STARTTLS, authenticated with the app password from the env var named in config
SendGridProvider POSTs to https://api.sendgrid.com/v3/mail/send with the API key from the env var named in config as a Bearer header
scripts/notify/notify.py imports only the Python standard library — zero new third-party dependencies
A missing secret env var causes the send to be skipped with a stderr warning naming the variable and exit 0
The resolved secret value never appears in any stdout/stderr line (asserted by a test)

entries.spec_requirement_id: project-email-notifications/Notification event types and per-recipient filtering
entries.summary: Five event types with per-recipient subscription filtering and context-bearing subject/body
scenarios: Event reaches only subscribed recipients
The "all" shorthand subscribes to every event
Unknown event type is rejected without sending
Event context appears in the email
acceptance_criteria: Exactly five event types are recognized: phase_start, phase_complete, issue_discovered, git_commit, deploy
A dispatched event reaches only recipients whose events array includes that type or the shorthand "all"
An unknown event type produces a stderr error, sends nothing, and exits 0
Each event's subject and body include its context — deploy includes the layer, git_commit includes the commit SHA, issue_discovered includes the issue summary, phase events include the phase name

entries.spec_requirement_id: project-email-notifications/Notifier CLI and best-effort failure isolation
entries.summary: argparse CLI at scripts/notify/notify.py; every failure path exits 0; module importable for tests
scenarios: Successful event emission via the CLI
Provider/network error never escalates
Invalid CLI arguments do not block the pipeline
Module is importable for testing
acceptance_criteria: scripts/notify/notify.py runs as `python scripts/notify/notify.py <event> [options]` accepting --project, --phase, --summary, --commit, --layer, --config
Every failure path — missing config, missing secret, provider error, network error, invalid arguments — results in exit code 0
The module is importable by pytest and exposes callable config/provider/dispatch/notify entry points without running the CLI
main() never returns a non-zero exit code

entries.summary: architect-team-pipeline skill emits all five events; wiring declared best-effort/non-blocking; structural test
scenarios: Skill body emits every event type
Wiring declares notifications non-blocking
Structural test enforces the wiring
acceptance_criteria: skills/architect-team-pipeline/SKILL.md contains a notifier invocation for each of phase_start, phase_complete, issue_discovered, git_commit, deploy
The wiring text states notifier invocations are best-effort and never block or fail a pipeline run
commands/architect-team.md notes the project email-notification feature
tests/test_notify_wiring.py asserts the five notifier invocations and the non-blocking statement are present in the pipeline skill

entries.summary: pytest coverage of config/providers/dispatch/CLI/failure with smtplib + urllib mocked
scenarios: Notifier test file exists and covers the module
Provider tests perform no real I/O
Full suite passes with the new tests
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_notify.py exists and covers config load/validate, Gmail + SendGrid message construction, event dispatch with per-recipient filtering, secret resolution, CLI parsing, and failure isolation
smtplib.SMTP and urllib.request.urlopen are mocked in provider tests — no real SMTP or network connection is opened
python -m pytest -v passes from the repo root with all new notifier tests and no regression in the pre-existing 431 tests

entries.summary: README + CHANGELOG + maps + CLAUDE.md updated; version bump 0.9.17 to 0.9.18
scenarios: README documents the feature
CHANGELOG and version are consistent
Maps reflect the new module and integrations
acceptance_criteria: README.md contains a section documenting the email-notification feature, the .architect-team-notify.json schema, the five event types, and env-var secret handling
CHANGELOG.md has a `## [0.9.18]` entry referencing the project-email-notifications requirements
.claude-plugin/plugin.json and .claude-plugin/marketplace.json both report version 0.9.18
docs/CODEBASE_MAP.md documents the scripts/notify/ module and .architect-team-notify.json; docs/INTEGRATION_MAP.md documents Gmail SMTP + SendGrid API; CLAUDE.md mentions the notifier

reuse_decisions.rationale: No existing email/notification capability anywhere in the codebase — repo-wide grep for `smtplib|sendgrid|notifier|send_email` returns zero hits, and docs/INTEGRATION_MAP.md lists no email integration. There is nothing to extend, compose, or reuse for the capability itself. Reuse is applied at every other level (see below).
reuse_decisions.codebase_map_anchor: docs/INTEGRATION_MAP.md external-integration list; docs/CODEBASE_MAP.md §3 directory tree + §4 Module Guide

reuse_decisions.rationale: The notifier is placed under the existing scripts/ directory as scripts/notify/notify.py (sibling to scripts/setup/setup.py), invoked as a path-addressed script exactly like setup.py — no new top-level directory. Event wiring extends the existing skills/architect-team-pipeline/SKILL.md; tests extend the existing pytest suite. No new skill, agent, command, or hook — the EXPECTED_SKILLS/AGENTS/COMMANDS registries and hooks/hooks.json are untouched.
reuse_decisions.codebase_map_anchor: docs/CODEBASE_MAP.md §3 (scripts/setup/), §4 Setup scripts, §4 Skills, §8 navigation guide

reuse_decisions.rationale: Transport uses only the Python standard library — smtplib + email.message + ssl for Gmail, urllib.request for SendGrid — adding zero third-party dependencies, mirroring the archived python3-portability change's 'no new dependencies' outcome. Config is JSON via stdlib json, the format already hand-edited for hooks.json and plugin.json; no YAML parser is introduced.

notes.no_frontend: The plugin has zero frontend surface; Playwright user-flow tests, DESIGN_MAP, visual-fidelity, and editability reviews do not apply.
notes.no_backend_api: The plugin has zero HTTP API surface; the feature is a CLI + library. Test discipline = pytest unit + structural. Gmail SMTP and the SendGrid HTTP API are genuinely external and non-deterministic, so they are the only mocked surfaces (smtplib.SMTP, urllib.request.urlopen) per dev-api-integration-testing's 'mock only what is truly external'. No requirement is layer=both, so no front-to-back integration criterion applies.
notes.teams: Two sequential task groups, one teammate each. notify-core (REQ-001..004, 006) owns scripts/notify/notify.py + .architect-team-notify.example.json + tests/test_notify.py. notify-wiring-docs (REQ-005, 007) owns skills/architect-team-pipeline/SKILL.md + commands/architect-team.md + tests/test_notify_wiring.py + README.md + CHANGELOG.md + docs/CODEBASE_MAP.md + docs/INTEGRATION_MAP.md + CLAUDE.md + .claude-plugin/plugin.json + .claude-plugin/marketplace.json. File scopes are non-overlapping; notify-wiring-docs depends on notify-core (the CLI must exist to wire and document it), so the groups run sequentially — no reconciliation phase needed.
